task_completion.description: Create ADR-001, ADR-002, and ADR-004 with correct tags, custom fields, and headings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing these tiddlers in view or edit mode. They must have the tag 'ADR', a custom field 'adr-status' (Accepted for 001/002, Proposed for 004), and text bodies containing the headings '!! Context', '!! Decision', and '!! Consequences'.

task_completion.description: Create ADR-003 with Deprecated status and a link to ADR-005.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for ADR-003 in the TiddlyWiki interface. It must have the 'ADR' tag, an 'adr-status' field set to 'Deprecated', and contain a wikitext link exactly matching '[[ADR-005: Migrate to Microservices Architecture]]' along with text stating it was superseded.

task_completion.description: Create ADR-005 with Accepted status and a link to ADR-003.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for ADR-005 in the TiddlyWiki interface. It must have the 'ADR' tag, an 'adr-status' field set to 'Accepted', and contain a wikitext link exactly matching '[[ADR-003: Use Monolithic Deployment]]' along with text stating it supersedes it.

task_completion.description: Create the 'ADR Index' tiddler with the 'ADR' tag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a tiddler titled 'ADR Index' being created or viewed. It must include the 'ADR' tag.

task_completion.description: The ADR Index contains the three required status headings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'ADR Index' tiddler's content (in edit or view mode). It must contain the headings 'Accepted', 'Proposed', and 'Deprecated' (e.g., written as '!! Accepted' in wikitext).

task_completion.description: The ADR Index uses <$list> widgets to dynamically display ADRs under each heading.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'ADR Index' tiddler in edit mode to verify the presence of <$list> widgets with filter syntax (e.g., '[tag[ADR]adr-status[Accepted]]'), OR look at the rendered view to see the correct ADR titles successfully listed under their respective status headings.

integrity.description: Agent used the TiddlyWiki web interface to create tiddlers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the TiddlyWiki UI in a web browser, with tiddler edit forms, tag dropdowns, and field input boxes being actively used, rather than terminal commands or text editor scripts.

integrity.description: Agent generated realistic sentences for the Context, Decision, and Consequences sections.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text under the headings in the ADR tiddlers should contain 1-2 actual sentences relevant to the specific ADR title, not just empty space, gibberish, or identical copy-pasted placeholder text across all ADRs.

integrity.description: Agent used dynamic list widgets rather than hardcoding the index.
integrity.visual_evidence: In the ADR Index edit view, ensure the agent typed <$list> widget code rather than manually typing out static bullet points for ADR-001, ADR-002, etc.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected ADRs and their statuses: ADR-001 (Accepted), ADR-002 (Accepted), ADR-003 (Deprecated), ADR-004 (Proposed), ADR-005 (Accepted). The ADR Index must use TiddlyWiki <$list> widgets to dynamically filter these based on the 'adr-status' field.
